text.title: Market Sizing Triangulation Method
text.summary: Top-down and bottom-up triangulation for TAM/SAM/SOM with confidence ratings and explicit assumption documentation.

rule.statement: Always run both top-down and bottom-up estimates; accept the result only when they are within 2-3x of each other.
rule.rationale: A single-path estimate cannot self-validate. When both independent models converge, confidence increases; divergence signals a data or assumption problem that must be resolved before presenting numbers.

rule.statement: Document every assumption explicitly with a source or a flag ("estimate, unvalidated"); never let an undocumented assumption enter an investor deck.
rule.rationale: Investors will ask the source for every number. If the answer is "we assumed it," the credibility of the entire model collapses. Explicit flags give the presenter a defensible answer.

rule.statement: Assign a confidence rating (H/M/L) to every data point: H = multiple independent sources agree; M = single credible source or partial agreement; L = single low-quality source or paywalled.
rule.rationale: Confidence ratings force honest calibration and let stakeholders assess which inputs to probe further.

rule.statement: Refresh market size estimates at minimum annually; flag any data point older than 18 months in fast-moving sectors as potentially stale.
rule.rationale: Market data is point-in-time. AI tools do not flag staleness automatically; the researcher must enforce the policy.

list.item: Perplexity Pro — best for synthesized multi-source estimates with citations; use for initial data gathering
Statista — market reports; summaries free, raw data paywalled; API for enterprise
CB Insights — company data, trends; API available for enterprise
AlphaSense — industry research and financial filings; strong for public company benchmarks
SimilarWeb — traffic and user estimates for digital market sizing; API available
ChatGPT / Claude — calculation assistance; no live data, hallucination risk for market figures

example.note: Top-down: Global task management software market = $4.5B (Statista 2025, H confidence). Segment: B2B SMB = 35% = $1.58B. Addressable with current feature set = 20% = $316M SAM.
Bottom-up: 2M SMBs in North America × 15% adoption rate × $1,200 ACV = $360M TAM. Converges within 2x of top-down. Midpoint: $338M. Confidence: M (adoption rate is estimated).

antipattern.description: Using ChatGPT or Claude as the primary source for a market figure without requiring a citation URL.
antipattern.reason: LLMs frequently fabricate specific market numbers that sound plausible but have no primary source; they cannot cite a page they did not read.

text.reference: Three-step TAM/SAM/SOM prompt with triangulation and confidence output
